data.description:
## Context

Pre-commit hooks today are syntactic only (paths, schemas, ticket IDs, executable bits). Several recurring failure classes have no static-detection gate and recur across the codebase weeks/months apart in different files, indicating a missing systematic check rather than per-script oversights. The release gate (`scripts/release.sh` precondition #8 → `validate.sh --ci`) catches these post-merge; the pre-commit stack admits them.

This epic adds four new pre-commit hooks that statically scan for known antipattern classes, each with a baseline-file mechanism so existing violations do not block rollout (descriptive-first ratchet, modeled after the 26-file SIGPIPE remediation series).

See `docs/observed-failure-patterns-4-26.md`, `docs/process-gaps-4-26.md`, and `docs/new-epics-4-26.md` for the full review and mitigation provenance.

## Cited evidence

**Pattern 1 — Environment hermeticity violations (~25% of recent fixes):**
- `fe45-0b58` (commit `dcbe98f3ef`): `skip-review-check.sh` references `$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT` under `set -u` → unbound variable → 27/56 tests fail in clean env
- `09d8-11f0`, `a190-d780` (commit `e913a71d5a`): `pre-commit-format-fix.sh` same pattern — 5/11 tests fail; identical root cause to `fe45-0b58` two months earlier
- `82ad-7bb3` (commit `f5a055d1fa`): `bash-runner.sh` does not export `CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT` before sourcing suite-engine
- `97a7-4504` (commit `495c8c4b4f`): `bash-runner.sh` fallback path needed annotation under plugin-self-ref check
- `4191-9096`, `0a47-9631` (commits `2261fcc83b`, `0968b46917`): `declare -A` (associative array) in scripts that must run on bash 3.2 (macOS default)
- `a40e-ab52` (in flight): `check-tickets-boundary.sh` resolves `BASH_SOURCE` via pre-commit's symlinked temp path → silent docs/ exclusion failure

**Pattern 4 — `set -o pipefail` + SIGPIPE on `… | grep -q` (~8%, Linux-strict):**
- `e241-41b2`: 50+ instances replaced with here-strings (`grep -q "$y" <<< "$x"`) across 26 files in commits `b9358f95eb` + `0113ab66d9`
- `999e-cf69`: a 27th instance later discovered in `ticket-push-rebase-conflict` after the 26-file sweep
- Current state: a fresh `grep -r "echo .* | grep -q" plugins/dso/ --include="*.sh" -l | wc -l` returns **42 files** still containing the antipattern, including `pre-commit-review-gate.sh:259` (a hook script — production code path)

**Pattern 7 — Spec / documentation gaps:**
- `a190-d780`: `commands.test_dirs` config key undocumented; CI's `test-config-keys-documented.sh` caught this post-merge

**Pattern 3 — Identical-root-cause regressions (~10%, cross-cuts Pattern 1):**
- The CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT chain `09d8-11f0` → `fe45-0b58` → `82ad-7bb3` → `97a7-4504` is at least four separate tickets with identical mechanical fixes; each was treated as a single-file fix and excluded from prior-art search by `prior-art-search.md:79-88` ("Routine exclusions: single-file logic fixes...")

**Gap evidence:**
- G1 (no hermeticity gate): all 18 hooks in `.pre-commit-config.yaml` are syntactic; `check-portability.sh` flags only `/Users/`, `/home/` hardcoded paths
- G6 (prior-art search excludes single-file fixes): verified at `prior-art-search.md:79-88`
- G11 (config keys decoupled from docs): no hook in `.pre-commit-config.yaml` cross-references config keys against `CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md`
- G12 (SIGPIPE has no static-detection gate): shellcheck does not flag this pattern; 42 files currently in violation

## Mitigations included

- **G1-M1** — `check-hermeticity.sh` pre-commit hook scanning staged `.sh` files for: (a) unguarded `$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT` references when `set -u` is active; (b) `declare -A` in scripts under directories the platform matrix targets (`plugins/dso/scripts/`, `plugins/dso/hooks/`, `.claude/scripts/`); (c) `BASH_SOURCE` resolution that does not flow through `readlink -f` or equivalent
- **G6-M2** — `check-known-antipatterns.sh` reading regex/AST queries from `plugins/dso/config/known-antipatterns.yaml`; append-only registry populated over time as new antipatterns are discovered (E receives them from fix-bug)
- **G11-M1** — `check-config-key-docs.sh` cross-referencing `dso-config.conf` keys against `CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md` (parses both, fails on missing entries)
- **G12-M1** — `check-pipefail-grep.sh` flagging `echo … | grep -q` and `printf … | grep -q` under scripts with `set -o pipefail` or `set -euo pipefail`; ships with baseline of existing 42 instances

## Proposed success criteria

1. Four new pre-commit hooks ship in `.pre-commit-config.yaml`, ordered between `shellcheck` and `pre-commit-review-gate`
2. Each hook supports a baseline file mechanism so existing violations do not block rollout; new violations are blocked
3. Hooks emit per-file diagnostics with the exact remediation string and a citation back to the originating ticket (`e241-41b2`, `a190-d780`, etc.)
4. `plugins/dso/config/known-antipatterns.yaml` is created, append-only, and documented in `CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md`
5. Suppression annotations follow the precedent of existing hooks (`# bash4-only:`, `# antipattern-ok: <reason>`); each annotation requires a justification token
6. Test coverage: each hook has a fixture-based test asserting (a) the antipattern is detected on staged content, (b) baselined existing violations do not fire, (c) new instances of the antipattern are blocked

## Coordination notes

- Coexists with `cf7b-86a9` (which adds shellcheck/ruff parity) — different layer, different antipattern class
- Sets up the registry that **New Epic E** (fix-bug recurrence) populates over time
- Reviewer rubric items in **New Epic B** (G1-M2, G11-M3, G12-M2) are the human-loop counterpart to these mechanical hooks

## References

- `docs/observed-failure-patterns-4-26.md` Patterns 1, 3, 4, 7
- `docs/process-gaps-4-26.md` G1, G6, G11, G12
- `docs/proposed-mitigations-4-26.md` G1-M1, G6-M2, G11-M1, G12-M1
- `docs/new-epics-4-26.md` § New Epic A
data.title: Static-analysis defense layer for hermeticity, antipatterns, and config drift
